zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Margaret Jackson於1578年5月19日出生在英國越可爾爾斯同時，她的父親William Jackson33歲，母親Elizabeth Wytham31歲。她於1597年與Sir Thomas Rippon Ripley在英國英格蘭波克夏聖 包利斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1660年7月17日在英國英格蘭越可爾包藍德享年82歲，埋葬在英國越可爾博爾頓 白 包藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Margaret Jackson于1578年5月19日出生在英国越可爾爾斯同时，她的父亲William Jackson33岁，母亲Elizabeth Wytham31岁。她于1597年与Sir Thomas Rippon Ripley在英国英格兰波克夏聖 包利斯 邱奇结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1660年7月17日在英国英格兰越可爾包藍德享年82岁，埋葬在英国越可爾博爾頓 白 包藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Jackson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: patronymic from Jack . In North America, this surname has absorbed other patronymics beginning with J- in various European languages, in particular those derived from equivalents or short forms and other derivatives of the personal name Jacob , e.g. Norwegian Jacobsen or Jakobsen and, in some cases, Slovenian Jakše (from a derivative of the personal name Jakob ). This surname is also very common among African Americans (see also 2 below).

African American: from the personal name Jackson (or Andrew Jackson), adopted in honor of Andrew Jackson, the 7th president of the US; or adoption of the surname in 1 above, in many cases probably for the same reason.

History: This extremely common British name was brought over by numerous different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One forebear was the father and namesake of the seventh US president, Andrew Jackson, who migrated to SC from Carrickfergus in the north of Ireland in 1765. The Confederate General Thomas ‘Stonewall’ Jackson came from VA, where his great-grandfather John, likewise of Scotch–Irish stock, had settled after emigrating to America in 1748.

variants.name: Jack
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Jak, Jakke, Jagge, Jake, Jeke, Jegge, a Picard-Flemish denasalized form of Old Picard and Middle Dutch Janke, a pet form of Jan (see John ). It was introduced by Flemings and Picards into Norman and Anglo-Norman usage, whence it became a common English and Scottish pet form of John. Although the surname is mainly Scottish in distribution, it also occurs in England, though the more common form there is Jackson .

English: occasionally perhaps from a Middle English borrowing of the Old French personal name Jacque(s) (James). However, it is uncertain whether English Jack was ever used as an alternative to James.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Jack (see 1 above) as a surname.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Margaret Jackson was born on 19 May 1578, in Rylstone, Yorkshire, England, her father, William Jackson, was 33 and her mother, Elizabeth Wytham, was 31. She married Sir Thomas Rippon Ripley in 1597, in St Paul's Church, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 17 July 1660, in Bowland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 82, and was buried in Bolton by Bowland, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Margaret Jacksonが1578年5月19日にイギリスヨークシュアイアリルストーンで生まれた時，父William Jacksonは33歳，母Elizabeth Wythamは31歳であった。1597年にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアスト プアウル'ス チャーチでSir Thomas Rippon Ripleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1660年7月17日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアボウランドで82歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアボルトン ビー ボウランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Margaret Jackson(이)가 1578년 5월 19일 영국 요르크실르 릴스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Jackson(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Wytham(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1597년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 스트 폴'스 c후르치에서 Sir Thomas Rippon Ripley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1660년 7월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 보우랜드에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 볼턴 비 보우랜드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Rippon Ripley em 1597, em St Paul's Church, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Margaret Jackson родилась 19 мая 1578 г. в местности рилстоун, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Jackson было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Wytham, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Rippon Ripley в 1597 г. в местности ст поул'с чурч, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 17 июля 1660 г. в местности боуланд, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 82 лет и была похоронена в местности болтон би боуланд, еркшир, Англия.

es.19 May 1578–17 July 1660: 19 de mayo de 1578–17 de julio de 1660
es.St Paul's Church, Berkshire, England, Un: St Paul's Church, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial: Old Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial America
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Margaret Jackson nació el 19 de mayo de 1578, en Rylstone, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Jackson, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Wytham, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Rippon Ripley en 1597, en St Paul's Church, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 17 de julio de 1660, en Bowland, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultada en Bolton by Bowland, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
